conditions.dose_amount: 500 µl applied within a plastic template (diameter: 2.5 cm), occluded for 30 minutes

evidence_items.snippet: A saturated solution of ibuprofen in propylene glycol/water was applied to the forearm of human volunteers for 30 minutes.

Drug application involved a saturated solution (~10 mg/ml) in 75:25 (v/v) propylene glycol/water. The solution applied (500 µl) was constrained by a plastic template (diameter: 2.5 cm) to prevent spreading and spillage, and was covered with an occlusive bandage during the 30-minute administration period.
This investigation examined the clearance phase of a model drug from the SC after a short application of an infinite dose.
Drug application involved a saturated solution (~10 mg/ml) in 75:25 (v/v) propylene glycol/water.
The integrated amounts in the SC were derived from the areas under the profiles in Figure 3 and yielded a mean (± SD) value of 12.2 (±3.5) µg/cm2.

Table II: Quantity of ibuprofen (mean ± SD, n=5) in the SC following a 30-minute application of a saturated drug solution in 75:25 (v/v) propylene glycol/water under different clearance conditions... Delay (h): 0 → 13.6±4.1; 0.5 → 17.4±0.9; 1 → 10.2±1.8; 2 → 9.3±1.4; 3 → 10.5±4.6; 4 → 9.7±2.9; 0.5 (occluded) → 7.3±1.0 µg/cm2.
ibuprofen DPK measurements were then conducted on the ventral forearms of the same volunteers.
The solution applied (500 µl) was constrained by a plastic template (diameter: 2.5 cm)
The site was then either stripped immediately or left unoccluded for 30 minutes or 1, 2, 3 or 4 hours before stripping.

provenance.route_notes: In vivo DPK (tape-stripping) study; no diffusion cell used. Ibuprofen applied topically in propylene glycol/water vehicle; SC concentrations measured by HPLC after tape-stripping. Study measures dermatopharmacokinetic clearance from SC, not transdermal flux. No Franz cell or synthetic membrane involved.

route_anchor_evidence.locator: Table II (page 19) and Figure 3 insert: ibuprofen quantity in SC (µg/cm2) at various clearance time points; partition coefficient K, diffusivity D/L2, AUC reported

saturated drug solution in 75:25 (v/v) propylene glycol/water applied for 30 minutes

route_raw_labels.barrier_name_raw: human stratum corneum (in vivo tape-stripping DPK)
route_raw_labels.where_endpoint: Table II (page 19) and Figure 3 insert: ibuprofen quantity in SC (µg/cm2) at various clearance time points; partition coefficient K, diffusivity D/L2, AUC reported
route_raw_labels.endpoint_carrier_snippet: Table II: Quantity of ibuprofen (mean ± SD, n=5) in the SC following a 30-minute application of a saturated drug solution in 75:25 (v/v) propylene glycol/water under different clearance conditions. Delay (h) Ibuprofen (µg/cm2): 0 = 13.6±4.1, 0.5 = 17.4±0.9, 1 = 10.2±1.8, 2 = 9.3±1.4, 3 = 10.5±4.6, 4 = 9.7±2.9, 0.5 (occluded) = 7.3±1.0
route_raw_labels.formulation_carrier_snippet: saturated drug solution in 75:25 (v/v) propylene glycol/water applied for 30 minutes
route_raw_labels.notes: In vivo DPK (tape-stripping) study; no diffusion cell used. Ibuprofen applied topically in propylene glycol/water vehicle; SC concentrations measured by HPLC after tape-stripping. Study measures dermatopharmacokinetic clearance from SC, not transdermal flux. No Franz cell or synthetic membrane involved.

source_notes: This is an in vivo DPK tape-stripping study; not a traditional Franz-cell IVPT. No receptor medium or diffusion cell is used — drug amount in SC is the endpoint. Diffusion area estimated from template diameter 2.5 cm (π×1.25²≈4.91 cm²). The endpoint_main value (12.2 µg/cm²) is from Figure 3 (immediately post 30-min application); Table II values cover the subsequent clearance phase (post-removal, unoccluded unless noted). Both occluded and unoccluded post-removal conditions are reported in Table II.
